channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Govt and Tourism Federation Condemn Gachagua's 'Avoid Kenya' Remarks
item.description: The Kenya State Department for Tourism, through Principal Secretary Julius Bitok, has strongly condemned remarks by impeached deputy president Rigathi Gachagua urging foreign tourists and investors to...

item.title: Ontario MPP Stan Cho Faces Scandal Over Taxpayer-Funded Expenses
item.description: Ontario Progressive Conservative Party MPP Stan Cho has faced renewed scrutiny over his riding association expenses, which include thousands of dollars in meals, hotel stays, and luxury travel billed ...

item.title: Protests in Delhi Demand Statehood for Occupied Kashmir Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Hundreds of people, led by the chief minister of India-occupied Kashmir (IoK), staged a protest in Delhi demanding statehood for the region. The demonstration, which took place seven years after the a...

item.title: Akbayan Condemns China's Attack on Philippine Navy in Ayungin Shoal
item.description: Akbayan, a political party in the Philippines, has strongly condemned what it described as a 'barbaric' and deliberate attack by the China Coast Guard (CCG) on Philippine Navy personnel in Ayungin Sho...

item.title: Iran retaliates against US strikes, fuel prices rise to $4 a gallon as conflict escalates
item.description: A cycle of escalating violence between Iran and the US has resulted in at least three American service members killed since hostilities resumed a week ago. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IR...

item.title: Open AI Models: A Form of Healthy Competition, Not a Security Threat
item.description: The article discusses the inevitability of open AI models and argues against treating them as security threats. It highlights how companies like Anthropic and OpenAI, valued near $1 trillion, face com...

item.title: Southern California Faces Dual Threat of Heatwave and Tropical Storm Elida
item.description: Southern California is bracing for a dangerous heatwave that will last seven days, with temperatures potentially exceeding 100°F (38°C) in many areas. The National Weather Service has issued warnings ...

item.title: Andy Burnham officially becomes UK Prime Minister after Keir Starmer's resignation
item.description: Andy Burnham has been officially installed as the UK Prime Minister by King Charles III following Keir Starmer's resignation after two years in office. The Labour leader, who orchestrated a leadership...

item.title: Marathi Web Series Explores Mom vs Mon-in-Law Dynamics
item.description: The trailer of the Marathi web series 'Aga Aai Aaho Aai' highlights a compelling family drama centered around the conflict between a mother-in-law and daughter-in-law. Directed by Varun Narvekar and w...

item.title: Twin Sisters Pursue Occupational Therapy to Help Family Member with Cerebral Palsy
item.description: Sheryl Chepkirui and Beryl Chepkogei, twin sisters from Uasin Gishu County, enrolled at Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Mbuvo Campus for the March 2025 intake. Their decision to study Occupation...

item.title: Andy Burnham becomes UK's new prime minister after Keir Starmer's resignation
item.description: Andy Burnham, former mayor of Greater Manchester, has been sworn in as the United Kingdom's new prime minister following the resignation of Keir Starmer. Starmer, who led the Labour Party for just two...

item.title: Onomo Waterfront Hotel: A Stylish Stay in a Converted Heritage Warehouse
item.description: The Onomo Waterfront hotel, located in the historic Spilhaus Warehouse on Alfred Street in De Waterkant, blends historical architecture with modern comforts. Converted from a 1901 warehouse, the prope...

item.title: China's AI advancements could disrupt global markets and tech giants
item.description: The article explores the potential impact of a new Chinese AI model that threatens to disrupt major players like OpenAI and Anthropic. This development could have significant ramifications for global ...

item.title: Dana White Criticizes World Cup Production Team for Errors During Final
item.description: UFC president Dana White delivered scathing criticism of the World Cup production team following errors during the final match. In an Instagram post, White sarcastically questioned the producers' comp...

item.title: Coeliac diagnosis transforms relationship with food after rapid weight loss
item.description: Ben Hampton's life changed dramatically after a coeliac disease diagnosis following rapid weight loss and debilitating symptoms. The 31-year-old experienced extreme pain, headaches, migraines, stomach...

item.title: Prosecutors' Subpoena of Duterte's Records Marks Progress in Pursuit of Truth and Accountability
item.description: The article discusses how prosecutors have called the subpoena of former President Duterte's records a significant step toward achieving truth and accountability. This legal action is part of ongoing ...

item.title: Gentrification and Displacement in Elephant and Castle
item.description: The article discusses the redevelopment of Elephant and Castle in south London, highlighting the displacement of local businesses and communities due to a £500m regeneration project. Diana Sach, a dis...

item.title: Africa's Investment Shift: Big Deals vs. Middle Market Struggles
item.description: The article highlights how Africa's investment landscape is splitting into two distinct segments. While large-scale infrastructure deals and tech startups are attracting billions in capital, mid-sized...

item.title: Punjab Assembly Aims to Restrict Dual Nationals in Government Roles
item.description: A resolution has been submitted to the Punjab Assembly seeking a ban on the appointment of dual nationals to government service, citing concerns over allegiance and access to sensitive state informati...

item.title: Vocational training initiative empowers 100 out-of-school girls in Tanzania
item.description: A recent initiative by Orica funding has provided vocational training to 100 out-of-school girls in Tanzania, aiming to equip them with practical skills for sustainable employment. The program focuses...
